title: BertLMHeadModel with relative position embedding does not work correctly when use_cache is true
issue_summary: Relative-position BERT generation diverged between cached decoding and full recomputation because the relative distance tensor was wrong when past_key_values was supplied.

root_cause.summary: When use_cache was enabled, the relative position distance computation collapsed to zero-distance behavior instead of using the true query and key positions.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: Cached decoding yields outputs that differ from a full forward pass over the same effective sequence.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case models the cached step with an incorrect zero-distance bias and compares it against the full-sequence relative-position result.

issue_contract.trigger: Enable cached decoding while using relative position bias.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy cached path collapses relative-position distances to a constant zero-distance bias.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: Cached decoding diverges from full recomputation on the same effective sequence.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces collapse of relative-position distance handling in the cached path.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported symptom that cached decoding diverges from full recomputation.
